instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
What Is An Endorsement In Life Insurance Policy?
A life insurance policy is usually a long-term contract. When buying coverage, you must honestly declare all your details in the proposal form. Moreover, the coverage details, like the sum assured, term, riders (if any), premium payment frequency, etc., are also recorded on the proposal form. The proposal form becomes the basis of your insurance contract.
During the policy tenure, if there are any changes in your personal details or coverage terms, amendments can be made to the original proposal form to record such changes. This is where endorsement comes into the picture.
The endorsement of life insurance plans means making changes to the existing policy 1. These changes include alterations, additions or deletion of the details contained in the policy form.
How does an endorsement work?
Endorsement includes changes to your original life insurance policy. You can make such endorsements at any time during the policy tenure. After an endorsement, the coverage and/or the premium might change. The same change is updated in the policy, and you might have to pay an additional or reduced premium for the same.
Some of the endorsements that can be done in a life insurance policy are as follows –
Change your personal details like your name, address, communication information, etc.
Change in the nominee
Assigning the policy in someone else’s name
Adding or removing riders
Changing the premium paying frequency
Increasing or reducing the sum assured
Changing the policy tenure, if allowed
Opting for the conversion option, if available
How To Make An Endorsement?
Making an endorsement is simple. You just have to inform the insurance company of the changes you need to make. Make a written request to the insurer for the change you want. If the change involves an increase in the mortality risk, the insurer might ask you to undergo a medical test before allowing the change. If an additional premium is required, you have to pay the premium before the change is allowed.
Once the change is made, the policy is endorsed with the changes, and you get a copy of the same.
Things To Remember While Making Endorsement
When you are making an endorsement in your life insurance policy, here are some things that you should remember –
Once you make an endorsement, it stays valid till the coverage tenure. However, in some cases, the change might be for a specified time period. Once the period is over, the endorsement becomes ineffective. For instance, in the case of a rider, the rider might have coverage for a limited tenure. Once the tenure is over, the rider coverage stops, and the endorsement ceases to be effective.
In some endorsements, you might have to submit additional documents for the change to be effective. For instance, if you have changed your name and want to record the same in the policy, you would have to submit the name change affidavit and other supporting documents depicting the change of name.
After the endorsement has been made, keep a copy of the new policy document for further requirements. In the case of any dispute at the time of claims, the changed policy document acts as proof of endorsement and helps you get claim settlements.
Conclusion
Your insurance needs might not remain constant throughout the policy tenure. If the needs change, you might have to change your coverage too. Moreover, in the case of changes in personal details, you should update your policy accordingly. Changes and updates in the policy are done through endorsements offered by insurance companies. Endorsements are free of cost changes that you might make in the policy and update the coverage to your changing needs.
So, if you want to make changes to your policy, know-how endorsements in life insurance work and get the changes done so that your policy aligns with your financial needs.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions.
